summary: Verified the summer schedule migration (ACQUAPOWER Martedì 18:30/Daniele, ACQUAGAG Giovedì 18:30/Davide). Backend fully passes 6/6 pytest tests including idempotency after backend restart. Frontend deep-navigation to Lezioni tab was blocked by an unrelated pre-existing modal stack (announcements popups '10 NUOVI ISCRITTI' + '4 PROVE ATTIVATE') that keeps re-appearing after each close. However, no JS crashes/pageerrors occurred when the new activity types are loaded, and constants.ts is correctly mapping 'acquapower'→'AcquaPower' and 'acquagag'→'AcquaGag'.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal stack ('4 PROVE ATTIVATE!' / '10 NUOVI ISCRITTI!') keeps re-appearing after each 'OK, VISTO!' click, blocking the bottom tab bar. Cannot reach the Lezioni tab as admin. Pre-existing behaviour unrelated to current fix but blocks admin UX.

action_items: OPTIONAL (pre-existing, unrelated to this fix): Investigate why 'nuovi iscritti / prove attivate' notification modals keep re-appearing after being dismissed by admin, blocking tab navigation.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py auto-migration in startup_event is correctly idempotent — verified across full backend restart, lessons remain exactly 11 with martedì/giovedì 18:30 aquatiche and no 17:30 residue.
constants.ts correctly defines 'acquapower' and 'acquagag' with human-readable names 'AcquaPower' / 'AcquaGag' and colours — no risk of unmapped-activity crash in the UI.
SCHEDULE constant (server.py L482-496) and import_lessons helper (L7328) are aligned with the migration seed — good.

success_rate.frontend: N/A - blocked by unrelated modal stack; no JS crash observed

test_credentials: Admin danielebrufani@gmail.com / Mariavittoria23 (login works). Client testclient_kpi@test.com / Test123456 (login works, but shows 'attiva prova' gate).
context_for_next_testing_agent: GET /api/lessons returns exactly 11 lessons with martedì 18:30 acquapower/Daniele and giovedì 18:30 acquagag/Davide. No 17:30 residue. Idempotency verified via supervisor restart. Frontend admin tab-navigation is blocked by a stack of announcement modals unrelated to this fix — if you need to reach the Lezioni tab, either dismiss modals via a JS eval hack or use a fresh client account that hasn't triggered them.
rca of the issue: N/A - fix is correct and idempotent. The only remaining concern is a pre-existing modal-stack UX issue unrelated to the schedule migration.
